content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
July 26, 1989 
Robert Marshall, Treasurer 
Friends of Nolan 
P.O. Box 649 
Glendale, CA 
91209 
Dear Mr. Marshall: 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-382 
You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure 
provisions of the Political Reform Act.ll 
FACTS 
As an annual event, Assemblyman Pat Nolan hosts a tour of the 
state capitol. 
The event is not a political fundraiser and the 
individuals touring the capitol will be reimbursing Friends of 
Nolan for the cost of their plane fare, transportation and meals 
in connection with the capitol tour. 
QUESTION 
How should Friends of Nolan report the receipt of the 
reimbursement checks on the Form 490, Candidate and Officeholder 
Campaign Statement--Long Form? 
CONCLUSION 
Friends of Nolan should report the reimbursements on Schedule 
G, Miscellaneous Increases to Cash. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act requires elected officials to disclose all receipts. 
Receipts which are not contributions or loans must be reported on 
Schedule G, Miscellaneous Increases to Cash. 
(Section 84211(k) 
II Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 
18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


Robert Marshall, Treasurer 
Page 2 
and the 1989 "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions 
of the Political Reform Act," page 98, copy enclosed.) 
If you have further questions concerning this matter please 
call me at 916/322-5662. 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
/' 
I") OJ/A. 
" -, 
By: 
Mary Ann Kvasager 
~ 
Political Reform Consultant 


Maryann Kvasager 
NoLAN 
ASSEMBLYMAN 
41st DISTRICT 
June 20, 1989 
Fair Political Practices commission 
P.o. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 
Dear Mary, 
On July 10, 1989, Assemblyman Pat Nolan will take 
several individuals on a trip to Sacramento. 
These 
individuals will be writing checks to our campaign committee 
(Friends of Nolan, I.D.# 890-051) for the cost of their 
plane fare, transportation and meals. 
We would like a written opinion on how to correctly 
report these payments on our campaign report. These payments 
are not contributions, but instead are reimbursements for 
the actual expenses incurred which are being billed to 
Friends of Nolan. 
They would appear to miscellaneous 
increases to cash which would be reported on the Schedule G 
of FPPC Form 940. 
We do not want these payments to count 
against an individual's maximum 
contribution amount. 
Please contact us at: 
RDM:nb 
Friends of Nolan 
512 East Wilson, #200 
Glendale, CA 91206 
(818) 246-8044 
Robert Marshall 
Treasurer 
NOT PRINTFn OR MAli Fn AT TAYPIIV<=PC, <=VDCkICt:: 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Robert Marshall 
Treasurer 
Friends of Nolan 
512 East Wilson, #200 
Glendale, CA 
91206 
Dear Mr. Marshall: 
June 28, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-382 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on June 27, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
/1 
}~~ ,_. 
_" _ ,-.c 
lTeanne Pr i tchard 
Chief Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box R07 •
